last_item: Band-1.5: loud immediate alert when a scheduled backup fails (backup.last_failed metric + backup-failed default rule + Collector.enrichBackup populating both backup metrics from the store)
last_result: done — added the immediate backup-failure alert path. backup-stale (no success in 26h) already existed but (a) only catches staleness ~26h late, too slow for a durability emergency, and (b) its metric backup.last_age_seconds was silently always 0 because the real pg.Sampler never reads the backup tables. Added LastBackupFailed to telemetry.Snapshot + 'backup.last_failed' metric (telemetry+alert namespaces both), a backup-failed default rule (SeverityCritical, For:0 immediate, 6h cooldown, Op>=1), and Collector.enrichBackup which derives both backup metrics FROM THE STORE in SampleOnce (Collector holds the store; Sampler does not): ListBackups(ctx,1) newest terminal row → failed; LatestSuccessfulBackup → age. No backups yet → fields untouched (failed=0) so a fresh box isn't falsely alarmed. Tests: engine fires backup-failed on failed snapshot + recovers on success; collector covers failed-latest/success-latest/no-backups; metricValue table + exporter metadata + Snapshot.Value coverage extended. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues; adopted both suggestions (metricValue table row for new key; enrichBackup sets failed=0 unconditionally on success). Full Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build); web untouched.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) IN PROGRESS. Done: (1) surface 'last good backup was N ago' (Dashboard + Backups-page banner). (2) loud alert when a scheduled backup fails/stale: backup-failed rule + backup.last_failed metric + Collector.enrichBackup populating backup.last_age_seconds & backup.last_failed from the store. ***CAPSTONE DISCOVERED + NOW TOP 1.5 ITEM: the entire telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP IS DORMANT — nothing in `indiepg serve`/ListenAndServe calls Collector.SampleOnce, Engine.Evaluate, or scheduler.Start, so NO alert (incl. backup-stale/backup-failed) fires in production yet. Wiring it (sample→evaluate→notify via configured Pushover/webhook channels; seed DefaultRules idempotently on first run) is the load-bearing next item — see backlog. Notifier construction pattern: server/handlers_alerts.go:306-311; channels in store config key alertChannelsConfigKey via loadAlertChannels.*** Remaining 1.5: (3) nudge backups off-host — S3 recommended default, warn on local-only. (4) restore-test surfacing (handleRestoreTest is a 'not implemented' stub). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue (separate key namespace from telemetry/snapshot.go MetricKeys — see learnings). Backups: internal/backup/manager.go writes terminal result 'fail'/'success'; internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup). go/psql/pg_ctl are snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
